gene_description: Solute carrier family 24 member 5 (SLC24A5) — cation exchanger protein controlling melanin synthesis and skin pigmentation

source.title: SLC24A5, a putative cation exchanger, affects pigmentation in zebrafish and humans
source.finding: rs1426654 A111T variant explains ~27% of skin pigmentation variation in South Asians. Ancestral allele (A) predominates in African and East Asian populations; derived allele (G/threonine) nearly fixed in Europeans and associated with lighter skin.

GG.effect: Homozygous for threonine at position 111. Strong light skin pigmentation phenotype. Nearly 100% frequency in European populations. Associated with reduced melanin production and increased risk of sunburn and UV damage.
GG.recommendation: High risk for sun damage and skin cancer. Daily broad-spectrum SPF 30+ sunscreen recommended. Avoid peak sun hours (10am-4pm). Regular skin cancer screening advised.

AG.effect: Heterozygous for mixed alanine/threonine alleles. Intermediate skin pigmentation—lighter than ancestral phenotype but darker than homozygous light-skin carriers. Common in admixed populations.
AG.recommendation: Moderate sun protection recommended. Use SPF 30+ sunscreen and protective clothing in sun exposure. Periodic skin checks appropriate.

GA.effect: Heterozygous for mixed alanine/threonine alleles. Intermediate skin pigmentation—lighter than ancestral phenotype but darker than homozygous light-skin carriers. Common in admixed populations.
GA.recommendation: Moderate sun protection recommended. Use SPF 30+ sunscreen and protective clothing in sun exposure. Periodic skin checks appropriate.

AA.effect: Homozygous for ancestral alanine at position 111. Strong melanin synthesis and dark skin pigmentation. Predominant in Sub-Saharan African, East Asian, and Indigenous American populations. Increased photoprotection against UV damage.
AA.recommendation: Standard sun protection sufficient (SPF 15+). Lower risk for skin cancer but still at risk during intense sun exposure. Vitamin D synthesis may be reduced in high-latitude regions with low sun exposure.
